data.title: SentinelOne Exceeds Q4 Revenue Expectations, But Guidance Concerns Linger, Oppenheimer Says
data.description:
SentinelOne's fiscal Q4 revenue exceeded expectations and despite posting strong results near-term investor concerns persist due to lower-than-anticipated guidance for the next fiscal year, Oppenheimer said in a note on Thursday.

The company demonstrated strong enterprise adoptions with 1,411 customers generating over $100,000 in annual recurring revenue with 40% of these customers using three or more products, according to the note.

The firm said that the decision to retire the deception solution resulted in a $10 million annual recurring revenue loss and will impact near-term revenue figures. Additionally, the decline in dollar-based net retention signals lower expansion within existing customers.

Oppenheimer said SentinelOne continues to see competitive gains, particularly in managed security service provider partnerships and non-endpoint security solutions, while it is prioritizing AI investments and strengthening product offerings.

The brokerage has an outperform rating on SentinelOne's stock with a $32 price target.

data.title: Order.co Unveils New Payment Features, Enhancing Security and Spend Visibility
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 13, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Order.co, the world’s leading B2B Ecommerce Platform, today announced its enhanced payment capabilities that improve visibility, efficiency, and security for procurement, finance, operations, and accounting teams. The update also allows for new features that simplify tax compliance while delivering unprecedented control over vendor payments and budget tracking.

“Recent enhancements ensure that every transaction is accounted for correctly and in real-time,” said Alec Stonitsch, VP of Product at Order.co. “With built-in features like live budget vs. actual tracking, centralized and automated management of tax exemptions, and vendor-specific virtual cards, businesses can experience more efficient, stress-free purchasing.”

Key benefits and features that Order.co customers can expect include:

Real-time visibility and budget control: Track pacing in real time as transactions instantly reflect in the business budget, eliminating overspending and confusion across teams. Finance teams gain better insight into credit utilization and cash-back opportunities, making it easier to plan ahead.

Increased efficiency: Automate tax exemption calculations, removing the need to contact individual vendors manually. Rather than manually GL coding every line item for every purchase, Order.co automatically codes items in consolidated invoices across multiple vendor categories, including advertising and operational expenses. Granular, data-rich reporting through the Order.co platform helps businesses stay compliant with tax regulations and state tax audits.

Stronger security: Ensure payments are only processed with approved vendors with Order.co’s vendor-locked cards. In the event of a vendor data breach, the virtual card only needs to be updated with that vendor without disrupting other transactions. As a result, finance and accounting teams can save time and minimize stress knowing that their financial data is secure.

With these upgrades, professionals in back-office functions can maximize cash flow, reduce tax complexities, and keep their financial information safe from potential breaches. To learn more about Order.co or to request a product demo, visit https://www.order.co/

About Order.co

Order.co simplifies business buying by combining the ease of online shopping with the sophistication of world-class purchase order and AP automation. The result? Businesses cut costs and complexity with every order.

Hundreds of companies, like WeWork and Hugo Boss, leverage Order.co to centralize purchase-to-pay workflows, scale operations, and gain total control over spending – saving an average of 5% on products. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in New York City, Order.co has raised $50M in funding from industry-leading investors like MIT, Stage 2 Capital, Rally Ventures, 645 Ventures, and more.

Media Contact

Allison Reich

Senior Manager of Brand, Content & Enablement

Allison.reich@order.co

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 11 AM ET
data.description:
Wholesale inflation flattens out in February in another sign of easing inflation

Wholesale prices were unchanged in February, offering more evidence that inflation pressures eased somewhat in early 2025.

Jobless claims little changed, while impact from government spending cuts expected later this spring

Initial jobless claims fell by 2,000 to 220,000 in the week that ended on March 8, the Labor Department said Thursday.

Putin Aide Rejects Plan for Ukraine Cease-Fire

A Russian negotiator said a U.S.-backed proposal for a 30-day truce would help Kyiv to regroup, as the Kremlin declared it is close to ousting Ukrainian forces from its Kursk region.

IEA Says Trade Tensions Weigh on Oil Demand, Warns of Supply Surplus

The International Energy Agency said the macroeconomic conditions underpinning its oil-demand projections have worsened over the past month due to global trade tensions.

European Truck Stocks Fall After EPA Sets Rollback of Emissions Standards

Daimler Truck shares were hit the hardest, after the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency said it would reconsider truck-emissions regulations.

Consumer Angst Is Striking All Income Levels

Signs of weakness are showing up in spending on everything from basics to luxuries.

The French Power Player in Charge of Boosting 'Made in Europe'

President Trump's tariffs threaten to shake up European industry. Stéphane Séjourné is with saving it.

Europe's Idling Factories Eye New Start in the Military

Industrial production in the eurozone recovered at the start of a year that could herald a new dawn for its beleaguered manufacturing sector.

Tesla's Stock Still Isn't Cheap

The EV maker has shed nearly half its value but still looks expensive compared with tech and auto peers.

Shoppers Are Skimping on Cigarettes, Doritos and Twinkies

Convenience-store sales have fallen as consumers cut back on snacks due to rising prices and health concerns.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Thursday at 11 AM ET
data.description:
Trump Family Has Held Deal Talks With Binance Following Crypto Exchange's Guilty Plea

The discussions for a stake in Binance's U.S. arm come as founder Changpeng Zhao has pushed for the administration to pardon him for his own 2023 guilty plea.

Deutsche Bank Expects to Moderate Pace of Dividend Hikes Beyond 2025

The lender reaffirmed its target for capital returns to shareholders for the five-year period ending 2025, but said dividends beyond then will grow more moderately.

Tariff Threats May Slow Private Equity's Nearshoring Bets in Mexico

Uncertainty over new Trump administration trade policies force some investors to rethink Mexican deals.

How Medicaid Spending Reductions Affect Startups

Reduced federal spending would likely sharpen focus on technology to improve efficiency, potentially benefiting some venture-backed companies.

Generali Operating Profit Rises on Higher Premiums

Assicurazioni Generali said its operating profit rose on the performance of all its units over 2024 gross written premiums that increased more than expected.

Is Trump Taking a 'Liquidationist' Approach to the Economy?

The administration's view that damaging the economy now could help it later comes with little upside for investors.

Venture Investors Hoped for an IPO Recovery in 2025. Now It's On Hold.

Market volatility makes IPO pricing a "monumental challenge." Meanwhile, limited partners are under pressure as distributions dry up.

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Read about Generali, Bridgepoint and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

Banner Ridge Seeks $3.2 Billion for New Secondary Fund

The special situations and distressed-debt specialist aims to raise one of the largest vehicles to acquire secondhand private-credit fund stakes.

Private-Credit Firms Expand Restructuring Teams Amid Bankruptcy Surge

Lenders are shelling out up to $1.5 million for mid- and senior-level restructuring professionals following a rise in out-of-court restructurings and bankruptcies.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Thursday at 11 AM ET
data.description:
Oil prices tick lower as IEA warns trade tensions will undercut demand

Oil futures headed lower on Thursday after the International Energy Agency's monthly report underscored worries that rising global trade tensions could undercut energy demand.

U.S. Natural Gas Futures Fall

U.S. natural gas futures fell ahead of EIA storage data, as the market has stopped ignoring bearish fundamentals.

IEA Says Trade Tensions Weigh on Oil Demand, Warns of Supply Surplus

The International Energy Agency said the macroeconomic conditions underpinning its oil-demand projections have worsened over the past month due to global trade tensions.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise For Second Straight Week

U.S. crude oil inventories increased by 1.4 million barrels, with an increase in production and net imports, while gasoline stocks fell more than expected as demand picked up.

OPEC Sticks to Oil-Demand View Ahead of Output Hike

The cartel still expects demand to grow by 1.45 million barrels a day this year and 1.43 million barrels a day the next, bolstered by strong air travel demand and road mobility.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Contact Energy, Harbour Energy and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

Wind and Solar Overtake Coal Power for First Time in U.S.

Natural gas remains by far the largest source of electricity in the country, according to research from clean-energy think tank Ember.

CEO Balks at Trump's Northeast Gas Pipeline Plan

The Williams Cos. CEO said on Tuesday that he isn't willing to undertake the project as long as the region's governors oppose it.

Clean-Energy Stocks Are Jumping as S&P 500 Dropped

Stocks of Enphase Energy, AES, and Nextera Energy gained ground. The industry has lagged behind the market for the past two years.

Energy Chief Pushes for Natural Gas Pipelines, Exports. Oil Slumps on Tariff Threat.

Chris Wright also signed a permit extension for a natural gas export terminal in Louisiana.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 11 AM ET
data.description:
Trump Family Has Held Deal Talks With Binance Following Crypto Exchange's Guilty Plea

The discussions for a stake in Binance's U.S. arm come as founder Changpeng Zhao has pushed for the administration to pardon him for his own 2023 guilty plea.

Donatella Versace to Step Down as Chief Creative Officer of Versace

Donatella Versace is stepping down as chief creative officer of the Versace brand, ending a more than quarter century run as the creative mind behind her family's high-end fashion house.

Mallinckrodt, Endo Merger to Create Diversified Pharmaceuticals Firm

Drugmaker Mallinckrodt struck a deal to buy Endo in an effort to build a diversified pharmaceuticals company with a hefty U.S. footprint.

Intel Names Lip-Bu Tan as Chief Executive Officer

The former CEO of Cadence Design Systems, steps in as the semiconductor manufacturer has lost market share.

Couche-Tard Doubles Down on Friendly Takeover of 7-Eleven Owner

The Canadian owner of Circle K will continue its friendly pursuit of Seven & i, executives said, sounding confident that they can overcome antitrust issues that could block the prospective acquisition in the U.S.

Dollar General Stock Jumps After Earnings. Shoppers Are Spending More on Essentials.

The company has more store closures planned for the first quarter.

Deutsche Bank Expects to Moderate Pace of Dividend Hikes Beyond 2025

The lender reaffirmed its target for capital returns to shareholders for the five-year period ending 2025, but said dividends beyond then will grow more moderately.

Accelleron Industries Exceeds $1 Billion Revenue for First Time

The engine-turbocharger maker's stock rose after it posted higher earnings, exceeded $1 billion in revenue for the first time and increased its dividend by 47%.

Generali Operating Profit Rises on Higher Premiums

Assicurazioni Generali said its operating profit rose on the performance of all its units over 2024 gross written premiums that increased more than expected.

Adobe Stock Slides Despite Earnings Beat. The CFO Is Confident About AI.

Adobe posted first-quarter adjusted earnings of $5.08 a share, beating estimates of $4.97 a share.

data.title: Silver at Over 4-Month High
data.description:
Silver climbed toward $33.50 an ounce, its highest level since late October, as investors sought safe-haven assets amid rising tariff tensions and growing bets on Federal Reserve rate cuts following weaker-than-expected U.S. inflation data.

Trade tensions escalated after President Trump threatened 200% tariffs on European wines, champagnes, and other alcoholic beverages in response to the EU’s 50% tariff on American whiskey.

Meanwhile, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick stated that a recession would be "worth it" to implement Trump’s economic policies.

On the economic front, U.S. producer prices were unexpectedly flat in February, while consumer inflation rose just 0.2%, below forecasts.

Additionally, jobless claims fell again last week, signaling a still strong labor market.

data.title: PTA-Adhoc: 4SC AG: 4SC Announces an Update to Resminostat's (Kinselby) Marketing Authorisation Application
data.description:
4SC AG

by Jason Loveridge

Public disclosure of inside information according to article 17 MAR Planegg-Martinsried (pta/13.03.2025/16:15) - The Management Board of 4SC AG ("4SC") (Frankfurt Stock Exchange, Prime Standard: VSC; ISIN: DE000A3E5C40) resolved today that after having consulted with both the broader 4SC team, external experts and its Supervisory Board it will proceed to formulate a response to the EMA's Day-180 Assessment Report, and in particular to its major objections, and intends to file this with the EMA within the required deadline. 4SC now expects to receive feedback from the Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) around the end of April 2025 and depending on the decision of the CHMP will then consider the future direction of the Company. ————————————————————————— Information and Explanation of the Issuer to this News: Forward-looking information Information set forth in this press release contains forward-looking statements, which involve risks and uncertainties. data.title: Romania's annual inflation inches up to 5.02% in Feb
data.description:
BUCHAREST (Romania), March 13 (SeeNews) - Romania’s consumer prices rose by 5.02% on the year in February, marking an uptick from a 4.95% annual increase in January, the statistical office said on Thursday.

The prices of services continued to rise at the fastest annual pace, of 6.42%, in February, the statistical office, INS, said in a press release.

Food and non-food prices increased by 4.54% and 4.79% on the year in February, respectively.

In February, Romania's consumer price index (CPI) edged up by 0.88% compared to January, with prices of food, non-food products and services rising by 0.73%, 1.08% and 0.66%, respectively.

The average annual inflation rate over the last 12 months was 5.2%.

In June 2023, the government decided to cap markups on some basic food products charged by food processing companies, distributors, retailers and cash-and-carry stores. In March, the parliament extended the 20% cap on the prices of basic foods until December 2024. On December 18, the government extended the markup cap on the prices of basic foods once again, until June 30, 2025.

On February 14, Romania's central bank, BNR, decided to keep its monetary policy rate unchanged at 6.5% for a fourth time since October, following a 25 basis-point (bp) cut in August.

In its February inflation report, the BNR raised its inflation projection for 2025 to 3.8% from 3.5% forecast in November, partly as a result of persistent pressures in the foods segment and still strong wage dynamics.

data.title: Intel Opportunity is Greater Under New CEO — Market Talk
data.description: Intel has a greater opportunity to restructure and turn things around the new leadership of Lip-Bu Tan, say analysts at BofA Securities in a research note. The beleaguered semiconductor company on Wednesday selected Tan, a former board member and longtime industry executive, as its new CEO. The analysts cite Tan's track record of success at Cadence Design Systems, among other factors in why they like the pick. They also think improving market conditions might help Intel divest certain assets, which would delever its balance sheet. "Intel's material turnaround remains a work in progress, but if executed well could be [an] incremental headwind for peer AMD," say the analysts. Shares surge 15% following the appointment. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

data.title: Spanish Commission Accepts Application Review for Borges Agricultural & Industrial Nuts
data.description:
Spain's National Securities Market Commission will process Borges International's application to launch a delisting takeover bid for the shares of Borges Agricultural & Industrial Nuts .

The commission said Thursday the application's admission for processing does not imply any type of decision relating to the authorization of the takeover bid.

Borges International applied for the delisting takeover of its nuts and dry fruits unit's shares on March 5.

data.title: Spire Global hits over four-month low after announcing $40 million private placement
data.description:
** Spire Global's shares fall as much as 11.5%, hitting their lowest since late October

** Virginia-based SPIR, a provider of space-based data, analytics and space services, announces $40 million private placement

** SPIR is selling about 5 million shares or pre-funded warrants at $8 apiece

** Purchase price represent a 22% discount to stock's last close

** Craig-Hallum Capital was the sole placement agent for the private placement

** SPIR has ~27.2 million shares outstanding for $279 million market cap - data compiled by LSEG

** Co will use the proceeds for working capital and general purposes

** Stock down 33% YTD after soaring 79.9% in 2024

data.title: European Equities Traded in the US as American Depositary Receipts Tread Water in Thursday Trading
data.description:
European equities traded in the US as American depositary receipts were treading water late Thursday morning, edging 0.1% higher to 1,396.7 on the S&P Europe Select ADR Index.

From continental Europe, the gainers were led by medical device maker EDAP TMS and pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk , which rose 4.7% and 3.3% respectively. They were followed by pharmaceutical company Ascendis Pharma and telecommunications operator Telefonica , which were up 1.7% and 1.5% respectively.

The decliners from continental Europe were led by semiconductor company Sequans Communications and software firm SAP , which fell 2.7% and 2.3% respectively. They were followed by biotechnology companies argenx and Evaxion Biotech , which dropped 2.1% and 0.9% respectively.

From the UK and Ireland, the gainers were led by biopharmaceutical companies Akari Therapeutics and telecommunications operator Vodafone Group , which increased 5.2% and 3% respectively. They were followed by pharmaceutical companies Amarin and AstraZeneca , which advanced 2.8% and 1.4% respectively.

The decliners from the UK and Ireland were led by biopharmaceutical company NuCana (NCNA) and Barclays (BCS), which lost 2.7% and 2.4% respectively. They were followed by biopharmaceutical companies TC Biopharm (TCBP) and Silence Therapeutics (SLN), which were down 1.8% and 1.2% respectively.

data.title: UiPath Is Maintained at Sell by UBS
data.description: (15:10 GMT) UiPath Price Target Cut to $10.00/Share From $14.00 by UBS

data.description:

data.title: 'Malört' Review: The Bitter Bottle That Beat the Odds
data.description:
By James Kennedy

Brutally bitter, with an aftertaste you can't shake, Jeppson's Malört is celebrated for its foulness. A specifically Chicago liquor made of grain alcohol and wormwood, Malört has been described as tasting like "stomach bile and dirt" and "a burning Band-aid at the bottom of a dumpster"; googling "Malört face" yields pages of suffering grimaces from first-time imbibers. Now it's the subject of Josh Noel's absorbing history "Malört: The Redemption of a Revered and Reviled Spirit."

Originally peddled door-to-door by Carl Jeppson in Chicago's Swedish-immigrant neighborhoods, Malört was sold as a medicinal remedy, exploiting a loophole in Prohibition-era laws that permitted alcohol so long as it was "unfit for beverage purposes." (Skeptical inspectors needed only to taste it to agree.)

In 1945 the elixir came under the ownership of George Broide, a son-of-immigrants striver who worked his way up to Northwestern University, a law degree and a lucrative career as a liquor executive — until he was nabbed for dodging the draft during World War II. Broide served time and was temporarily disbarred. Upon his release, he changed his name to Brode and pared his spirits business down to one odd drink: Malört. "He could have kept a brand more ordinary and obvious," writes Mr. Noel. "Where would the fun have been in that?"

A born hustler, Brode circulated among 1960s Chicago taverns hawking Malört as a masculine drink. "Are you man enough to drink our two-fisted liquor?" challenged one advertisement. "No woman wets her whistle on Jeppson," another crowed. Brode barely sold enough Malört to keep the operation going, but he had other sources of income. He promoted Malört for the love of the game. When he died in 1999, he bequeathed the company to Pat Gabelick, his secretary of 33 years and longtime mistress.

Ms. Gabelick never cared for Malört herself. Throughout the early 2000s she kept the company puttering along in Brode's memory — but on autopilot. There was "no sales team, no marketing, no advertising," writes Mr. Noel. Malört was still stocked at old-school bars, but as a drink bartenders kept on the shelf to punish annoying patrons who demanded a free shot — and for their own after-hours consumption. It's an acquired taste.

And tastes were changing. By 2008 the cocktail revival movement was gaining steam. After decades of obscurity, the time was ripe for the second act of the worst drink in the world. "Malört never changed to please the masses," writes Mr. Noel. "The masses changed to understand Malört."

Curious buffs tracked down Ms. Gabelick and were surprised to discover that she ran the company single-handedly out of her lakefront condo. Bemused, she tolerated the hipsters, although she doubted Malört's growing cachet. But sales were no longer exclusive to elderly Swedish immigrants. Adventuresome 20-somethings had a different relationship to Malört. "They wanted to revel in it," writes Mr. Noel. "To talk about it, to share it with friends, to laugh at it, to laugh with it." That is, Malört was perfect for social media.

Ms. Gabelick resisted putting up a website or even trademarking her product's name. Meanwhile, a young enthusiast named Sam Mechling was already doing guerrilla marketing for Malört: running edgy fan accounts, selling unauthorized T-shirts, holding live Malört-themed events. When Ms. Gabelick found out, she was canny. Instead of shutting Mr. Mechling down, she allowed him to act as Malört's unpaid social-media director.

With the help of two other Malört-loving volunteers, by 2012 the once-moribund liquor was selling better than ever and was a cultural force to boot. Anthony Bourdain said that Malört made him feel "stabby and belligerent." In the 2013 movie "Drinking Buddies," Jason Sudeikis described drinking Malört as "like swallowing a burnt condom full of gas."

Ms. Gabelick may have been cajoled into embracing 21st-century marketing tactics by her young volunteers, but their zeal paid dividends: From 2007 to 2017, Mr. Noel tells us, sales increased nearly 1,000%. Not bad for a drink once described as "hairspray and death."

Success brought new problems. Ms. Gabelick and her scrappy band had to fend off imitators, navigate trademark legalities and even face down an attempted takeover. Feelings got hurt, too. As Malört took off, relations between Ms. Gabelick and her team frayed; the volunteers felt their unpaid efforts were dismissed or underappreciated.

When Ms. Gabelick sold the company in 2018 to a local distillery for $2.2 million, Mr. Mechling was assigned to the sales team, but it didn't work out. He eventually moved back home to Ohio, giving up his MALORT vanity license plate. "For years it hurt," writes Mr. Noel. Talk about a bitter aftertaste.

Mr. Noel is a seasoned journalist for the Chicago Tribune and writes with winning fondness about the eccentrics, fanatics and everyday folks drawn into Malört's orbit. Along the way, he makes the story of Malört into the story of a changing Chicago. George Brode feels like a character from Saul Bellow's "The Adventures of Augie March," full of brash entrepreneurial energy — indeed, Bellow, we are told, was one of Brode's childhood neighbors. The decline of Malört's popularity during the second half of the 20th century tracks the decline of working-class Chicago; its resurgence, the gentrification of Nelson Algren's and Studs Terkel's hardscrabble neighborhoods into bougie playgrounds.

But Mr. Noel also affectionately portrays the openhearted, inventive spirit of the early social-media era that propelled Malört to profitable notoriety. In the end, his account of the bitter beverage has a hard-won sweetness. This quirky, enlightening, unexpectedly poignant book isn't only for aficionados of the offbeat Chicago liquor, but fans of stubborn underdogs everywhere.

Mr. Kennedy is the author, most recently, of "Bride of the Tornado."

data.title: Adobe Investors Need 'Clearer Roadmap' to Dispel 'Apathy' on Shares, Morgan Stanley Says
data.description:
Adobe investors need a "clearer roadmap" to dispel "apathy" surrounding the stock even after the software company's Q1 results topped estimates by analysts, Morgan Stanley said Thursday in a report.

"Investors look for greater visibility on three key focal points to gain confidence in the forward trajectory" of the company, including detailed data on annual recurring revenue for digital media, cloud and document cloud segments, as well as artificial-intelligence products, the report said.

The new disclosure of generative artificial intelligence metrics "is a step in the right direction," the report said.

"Management acknowledged volatility in the broader macro environment, but highlighted expectations for Adobe to remain relatively durable," Morgan Stanley said.

An Adobe investor summit is scheduled for Tuesday.

Morgan Stanley cut its price target on Adobe stock to $600 from $660 and maintained its overweight rating. Several other analysts reduced price targets.

Shares of Adobe fell 12% in recent Thursday trading.

data.title: Trump-linked small stocks pull back after rallying on family ties
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump's re-election prompted a run-up in the broad stock market that has since receded. The same has happened in a number of tiny companies linked to the president's two eldest sons.

In the last few months, Donald Trump Jr and Eric Trump have announced their involvement in several smaller companies ranging from the e-commerce, drone manufacturing and financial advisory industries.

The announcements sparked a frenzy of buying in those stocks - generally thinly traded names with tiny market valuations and minimal revenue - only to give back most of those gains.

Donald Jr and Eric Trump currently run the Trump Organization, the president's real estate company. Their association with smaller, publicly listed companies since the election indicates their interest in leveraging that win in the business world.

On February 24, the duo disclosed a combined 13.4% stake in biotech firm-turned-financial advisory Dominari Holdings , which is headquartered at the Trump Tower in New York. They were appointed to Dominari's board of advisers on February 11.

Shares doubled after the announcement, but are now down about 6% since their appointment. More than 23 million shares in the stock traded a day before the announcement, LSEG data showed. By contrast, average volume in 2024 was just 13,000 shares a day, according to Reuters calculations.

The Trump Organization and Dominari Holdings did not respond to requests for comment.

Drone maker Unusual Machines in November added Don Jr to its advisory board, who noted in a statement the need for America to "stop buying Chinese drones and parts." The company, however, said in a filing last year that it was heavily dependent on Chinese imports for products and operations.

Allan Evans, CEO of Unusual Machines, told Reuters the company was making progress on shifting its supply chain to domestic production, estimating that Chinese imports would account for less than 50% of the supply chain by year-end.

Shares jumped 260% on the news of Donald Jr's appointment, but have since fallen back. They are still up about 14% since the announcement for a market value of about $93 million.

Dominari and Unusual share another link. Dominari was an underwriter in Unusual's IPO in 2024 and was its placement agent when it raised about $2 million in October in a private fundraising. Evans said Donald Jr joined the company as an investor in that private placement. He owned 331,580 shares of Unusual as of December, according to regulatory filings.

Dominari reported revenue of $11.6 million in the first nine months of 2024, while Unusual posted about $3.6 million.

James Wood, CEO of short-selling researcher Differentiated Analytics, said the companies were benefiting from the name recognition. The firm has a short position in PSQ Holdings , which added Donald Jr to its board of directors in December.

"The apparent purpose of these appointments is to drum up investor hype. I thus expect the bump in the share price to be ephemeral," Wood said.

Michael Seifert, CEO of PSQ, which owns online shopping website PublicSquare, said Donald Jr's appointment was not due to hype, but a natural progression in his involvement with the company since first investing in it before it went public in July 2023.

The company bills itself as "anti-woke," with a goal of connecting shoppers to brands aligned with the conservative movement. Upon Donald Jr's appointment, PSQ stock rose as much as 270%, but has since given back most of those gains; it is up 22% since the announcement and is worth about $87 million.

data.title: Gold Prices Top New Record Levels
data.description:
Gold prices surged about 1% to exceed $1,970 an ounce on Thursday, hitting fresh record highs as risk aversion intensified.

Escalating trade tensions fueled the flight to safety, with US President Trump threatening a 200% tariff on wine and other alcoholic beverages from the EU in response to the bloc’s newly announced countermeasures.

Geopolitical concerns also weighed on sentiment, as Russia signaled its refusal to the 30-day ceasefire accepted by Ukraine and backed by the US. Meanwhile, uncertainty over the US economic outlook persisted, with traders weighing the potential for a recession amid the fallout from the new tariffs.

data.title: REG - Hemogenyx Pharma Plc - Conversion of convertible loan notes
data.description:
RNS Number : 6137A Hemogenyx Pharmaceuticals PLC 13 March 2025  

13 March 2025

Hemogenyx Pharmaceuticals plc

("Hemogenyx Pharmaceuticals" or the "Company")

Conversion of convertible loan notes and admission of shares

Hemogenyx Pharmaceuticals plc is pleased to announce that, further to its announcement on 19 February 2025, the convertible loan notes ("CLNs") automatically converted on 8 March 2025 into 95,000 ordinary shares ("CLN Shares").

An application has been made to the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange, and admission of the CLN Shares to trading is expected on 14 March 2025 ("Admission"). The CLN Shares will rank pari passu with the Company's existing Ordinary Shares.

Total Voting Rights

For the purpose of the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, following Admission the enlarged issued share capital of the Company will comprise 3,699,539 ordinary shares. The Company does not hold any shares in treasury. The above figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company, under the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

Market Abuse Regulation (MAR) Disclosure

Certain information contained in this announcement would have been inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of Regulation No 596/2014 (as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018) until the release of this announcement. The person responsible for arranging for the release of this announcement on behalf of Hemogenyx Pharmaceuticals plc is Dr Vladislav Sandler, Chief Executive Officer & Co-Founder.

Enquiries:

Hemogenyx Pharmaceuticals plc

https://hemogenyx.com

Dr Vladislav Sandler, Chief Executive Officer & Co-Founder

headquarters@hemogenyx.com

Peter Redmond, Director

peter.redmond@hemogenyx.com

SP Angel Corporate Finance LLP

Tel: +44 (0)20 3470 0470

Matthew Johnson, Vadim Alexandre, Adam Cowl

Peterhouse Capital Limited

Tel: +44 (0)20 7469 0930

Lucy Williams, Duncan Vasey, Charles Goodfellow

About Hemogenyx Pharmaceuticals plc

Hemogenyx Pharmaceuticals is a publicly traded company headquartered in London, with its US operating subsidiaries, Hemogenyx Pharmaceuticals LLC and Immugenyx LLC, located in New York City at its state-of-the-art research facility.

The Company is a clinical stage biopharmaceutical group developing new medicines and treatments to treat blood and autoimmune diseases. Hemogenyx Pharmaceuticals is developing several distinct and complementary product candidates, as well as platform technologies that it uses as engines for novel product development.

data.title: SentinelOne Is Maintained at Neutral by UBS
Mallinckrodt, Endo Enter $6.7 Billion Merger Deal as Drug Makers Plan New US Listing
data.description:
Mallinckrodt and Endo are set to merge in a cash-and-stock deal valued at $6.7 billion, with the drug makers set to list shares of the new firm on the New York Stock Exchange.

Shareholders in Endo, which makes sterile injectables and generic pharmaceuticals, will receive $80 million in cash and a 49.9% stake in the combined company on a pro forma basis. Investors in Dublin-based Mallinckrodt will hold a 50.1% interest. The deal requires approval from shareholders and regulators.

"This exciting combination will create a larger and more diversified entity with the scale and resources needed to unlock the full potential of both companies," Mallinckrodt Chief Executive Siggi Olafsson said in a statement Thursday. "With a strong pro forma balance sheet and compelling synergy opportunities, we will have greater flexibility to invest in innovation and pursue growth opportunities."

The new firm is expected to deliver $3.6 billion in pro forma 2025 revenue and $1.2 billion in pro forma adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization, Mallinckrodt and Endo said in the statement.

Endo, which currently trades over-the-counter, and Mallinckrodt have both filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy proceedings in recent years, as debts mounted and opioid-related legal challenges weighed. Through the deal, Mallinckrodt's existing senior secured term loans and notes will be refinanced, while Endo's debt remains outstanding, the companies said.

Olafsson will become CEO of the firm that will be based in Dublin, while Paul Efron, an Endo board member, will be chair. A spinoff of Endo's sterile injectables and both companies' generics business is also being planned, the companies said.

"The combined company will possess a branded business with the scale, cash flow and balance sheet strength to invest in both internal and external growth opportunities, including pursuing commercial-stage assets," said Scott Hirsch, Endo's interim CEO. "The stable and robust free cash flow generated by the combined sterile injectables and generics business should enable consistent capital returns to shareholders following its separation."

data.description: newsfile.refinitiv.com/getnewsfile/v1/story

data.title: Peoples Bancorp of North Carolina Approves $3 Million Share Repurchase Plan
data.description:
Peoples Bancorp of North Carolina said Thursday that its board has authorized a share repurchase program of up to $3 million.

The timing and amount of repurchases will be determined by management, based on market conditions and other factors, the company said.
